ticketTitle: Guard Score() with language-tier gate, protect complexity maintainability rule, remove 0.5 defaults, update pipeline, and fix all engine tests

nonGoals: Do not modify any existing files
Do not add tests beyond what the package naturally needs
Do not integrate with engine/scorer yet
Do not create any other files in the language_tiers package
deliverables.objective: Create internal/language_tiers/tiers.go with Tier type, TierGeneric/TierNative constants, and TierForLanguage mapping
deliverables.planText: Create internal/language_tiers/tiers.go. Define Tier as int type with const TierGeneric Tier = 0 and TierNative Tier = 1. Implement TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier that returns TierNative for 'go', 'typescript', 'python', 'rust' (case-insensitive via strings.EqualFold) and TierGeneric for everything else. Include a package doc comment and doc comments on the exported type, constants, and function.

nonGoals: Do not change any engine or pipeline code
Do not alter existing Grade constants or their values
Do not add new methods beyond updating the String() switch
Do not modify CertificationRecord fields other than adding Unsupported
deliverables.objective: Add GradeNA constant to domain/dimension.go and Unsupported bool field to domain/record.go
deliverables.planText: In internal/domain/dimension.go: add GradeNA Grade = 255 with string representation 'N/A'. Update the Grade.String() switch statement to return 'N/A' for GradeNA. In internal/domain/record.go: add Unsupported bool field to the CertificationRecord struct with json tag 'unsupported' and a doc comment explaining it indicates the unit's language has no parser available.

nonGoals: Do not modify pipeline.go
Do not change the Score() return type (still returns map[domain.Dimension]*domain.DimensionScore)
Do not alter scoring rules beyond the three specified changes (tier gate, complexity guard, violation else-branch removal)
Do not add new exported functions or types
deliverables.objective: Guard Score() with language-tier gate, protect complexity→maintainability rule, and remove 0.5-default violation injection in scorer.go
deliverables.planText: In internal/engine/scorer.go: (1) Add import for internal/language_tiers. (2) Change Score() function signature to accept lang string as first parameter before evidence and violations. (3) At the very top of Score(), call language_tiers.TierForLanguage(lang); if the result is TierGeneric, return nil immediately. (4) Change scoreFromMetrics() signature to accept lang string parameter. (5) In scoreFromMetrics(), locate the complexity≤5→maintainability=0.95 boost path; add a guard: skip the boost when complexity==0 AND language_tiers.TierForLanguage(lang)==TierGeneric (because 0 means 'unmeasured', not 'excellent'). (6) Remove the violation else-branch (approximately lines 59-61) that injects missing dimensions with a 0.80 penalty — violations should now only penalize dimensions that already exist in the scores map. (7) Update doc comments on Score and scoreFromMetrics to document the new lang parameter and TierGeneric behavior.

nonGoals: Do not modify production code
Do not add pipeline tests
Do not remove or weaken existing test assertions
Do not change the test suite structure beyond adding the language arg and new test functions
deliverables.objective: Update all existing Score() calls in scorer_test.go with language argument and add three new test cases for the changed behavior
deliverables.planText: In internal/engine/scorer_test.go: (1) Add 'go' as the language argument to every existing Score() call site to match the new signature. (2) Add TestScorer_TierGenericReturnsNil: call Score() with language 'swift' and valid evidence/violations, assert the returned map is nil. (3) Add TestScorer_ZeroComplexityTierGenericNoMaintainabilityBoost: construct evidence with complexity=0 and language 'swift', call Score(), and assert that either the maintainability dimension is absent from the result map OR its score is not 0.95. (4) Add TestScorer_ViolationOnAbsentDimensionDoesNotInject: create a violation targeting a dimension that has no corresponding evidence, call Score() with language 'go', and assert that dimension is absent from the returned scores map (the old 0.80-penalty injection no longer fires). Run go test ./internal/engine/... -count=1 to verify all tests pass.

nonGoals: Do not modify scorer.go
Do not change the CertifyUnit function signature or return type
Do not alter the pipeline flow for supported languages
Do not add new exported functions
deliverables.objective: Update pipeline.go to pass language to Score() and short-circuit with GradeNA/Unsupported when scores are nil
deliverables.planText: In internal/engine/pipeline.go: (1) Locate the call to Score() inside CertifyUnit (or whichever pipeline function invokes the scorer). Add unit.ID.Language() as the first argument to match the new Score(lang, evidence, violations) signature. (2) Immediately after the Score() call, add a nil check on the returned scores map. If scores == nil: set record.Grade = domain.GradeNA, set record.Unsupported = true, and return the record immediately — skip all downstream pipeline steps (risk adjustment, certification window, etc.) for this unit. (3) If domain is not already imported, add the import for internal/domain to access GradeNA.

nonGoals: Do not modify production code
Do not add scorer tests
Do not alter existing pipeline test cases
Do not introduce test helpers used by other tests
deliverables.objective: Add TestPipeline_CertifyUnit_UnsupportedLanguage to pipeline_test.go covering the GradeNA/Unsupported short-circuit
deliverables.planText: In internal/engine/pipeline_test.go: Add TestPipeline_CertifyUnit_UnsupportedLanguage. Create a unit whose ID.Language() returns 'swift' (or any non-TierNative language). Call CertifyUnit with appropriate context and evidence. Assert that the returned record.Grade equals domain.GradeNA and record.Unsupported is true. Run go test ./internal/engine/... -count=1 to verify, then go test ./... -count=1 for full-project regression.
